fuzzy_prd:
hey team, we need to get this email toolkit wrapped up for the transactional messaging service. basically devs are complaining they have to hand-wire everything themselves every time a new service needs to send emails — it's a mess and keeps breaking in prod when someone forgets a header or messes up an attachment. we want a clean library that handles building email objects, sticking files onto them, and rendering the body from templates without everyone having to learn a new engine from scratch each time.

the value objects need to blow up early if someone forgets something important — similar to how we handled the validation in that user-profile module, just make sure errors are specific enough that devs know exactly what they forgot. for images embedded in the body, there's a fixed set of supported kinds (check the existing catalog logic) and each one needs its mime type resolved automatically.

for templates we want to support a few different engines — the ftl one, a couple of html-based ones (there are at least three of those, one is the logic-less one, one does inheritance, and there's that natural-templating one). wrong extensions or blank names should fail clearly, and broken templates should also have their own distinct failure mode.

attachments should auto-detect content type from actual bytes when no type is given explicitly. the whole thing should be modular — no god files. prioritize getting the test harness wired up so we can validate everything end to end.

trigger_keywords: what fields are required for an email
which email fields are mandatory
what happens if I omit the sender
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The four mandatory fields for building an email value object are: 'from' (sender address), 'to' (at least one primary recipient), 'subject', and 'body'. Omitting any one of these must produce an error with exactly two lines: 'error=missing_required_field' and 'field=<fieldname>' where fieldname is one of: from, to, subject, body.

trigger_keywords: how are custom headers reported in output
what format are custom headers printed in
how do I output custom headers sorted
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Custom headers must be output sorted alphabetically by key. Each header appears as 'custom_header.<key>=<value>'. The count must appear first as 'custom_header_count=<n>'. When no custom headers are supplied the count is 0 and no individual header lines appear.

trigger_keywords: what image types are supported
which image kinds does the catalog include
what mime types map to the supported image formats
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The supported image kinds in catalog order are: GIF (extension=gif, content_type=image/gif), JPG (extension=jpg, content_type=image/jpeg), JPEG (extension=jpeg, content_type=image/jpeg), PNG (extension=png, content_type=image/png). The catalog must be listable in this exact order and each kind must be individually lookable by name.

trigger_keywords: what fields are required for an inline picture
which coordinates are mandatory for inline image descriptor
what error is thrown when inline picture fields are missing
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: An inline picture descriptor requires three mandatory fields: 'imageType' (must be one of the supported catalog kinds), 'file' (the backing file path), and 'templateName' (the placeholder name used in the body template). Missing any one produces error=missing_required_field with field= set to the missing field name: imageType, file, or templateName.

trigger_keywords: how is attachment content type resolved
when is mime type auto-detected for attachments
what happens when no media type is given for an attachment
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When an explicit 'mediaType' is supplied for an attachment it is used verbatim. When none is supplied the content type is auto-detected from the actual byte content combined with the file name. For example, a real JPEG image file resolves to 'image/jpeg'. The attachment must also expose resource_exists=true and resource_size=<byte count of the payload>. Omitting the attachment data entirely (no dataText, no sampleImage) yields error=missing_required_field with field=attachmentData.

trigger_keywords: what is the sample image used for attachment testing
how many bytes is the test JPEG
what is the bundled image for content type detection
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The toolkit must include a bundled real JPEG sample image used when 'sampleImage: true' is passed in the test adapter. This image must be exactly 59665 bytes and must auto-detect as content_type=image/jpeg. It is bundled as a classpath resource so that genuine byte-level content-type detection can be exercised.

trigger_keywords: how does mime assembly handle reply-to
what is the default reply-to when not specified
when is multipart set to true in mime output
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When no explicit replyTo address is provided, the reply_to field in the assembled MIME message output defaults to the sender (from) address. The multipart flag is set to 'true' exactly when one or more attachments are present; otherwise it is 'false'. When multipart=false the body text is included in the output as 'body=<text>'; when multipart=true the body line is omitted from the reported output.

trigger_keywords: what headers are emitted for read receipt
how is disposition notification reported in output
what are the wire header names for receipt and notification
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Read-receipt requests are reported as 'header_return_receipt=<address>' and disposition-notification requests as 'header_disposition_notification_to=<address>'. Both lines always appear in the output — when not requested they appear with empty values (e.g., 'header_return_receipt='). These correspond to the receiptTo and depositionNotificationTo input fields respectively.

trigger_keywords: what file extension does the freemarker engine expect
which template engine uses ftl extension
what placeholder syntax does the expression-and-directive engine use
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The FreeMarker (expression-and-directive) engine expects templates with the 'ftl' file extension and uses ${name}-style placeholders. A blank template reference or a reference whose extension is not 'ftl' yields error=invalid_template_name with engine=freemarker. A syntactically broken template (e.g., wrong_email_template.ftl) yields error=template_processing_failed with engine=freemarker.

trigger_keywords: what extension does the mustache engine require
which engine is the logic-less one
does mustache have a template processing failure case
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The Mustache (logic-less) engine expects templates with the 'html' file extension. A blank reference or wrong extension yields error=invalid_template_name with engine=mustache. The public test cases for Mustache do not include a template_processing_failed case, but invalid_template_name validation is required and identical in behavior to the other engines.

trigger_keywords: what extension does pebble use
which engine supports template inheritance
what happens with a broken pebble template
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The Pebble (inheritance-capable) engine expects templates with the 'html' file extension. A blank reference or wrong extension yields error=invalid_template_name with engine=pebble. A syntactically broken template (e.g., wrong_email_template.html) yields error=template_processing_failed with engine=pebble.

trigger_keywords: what extension does thymeleaf use
which engine is the natural-templating one
how does thymeleaf handle hyperlink targets in templates
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The Thymeleaf (natural-templating) engine expects templates with the 'html' file extension and supports attribute-level expressions for both text content and hyperlink targets (e.g., th:text and th:href). A blank reference or wrong extension yields error=invalid_template_name with engine=thymeleaf. The model can include an 'activationLink' variable that is substituted into anchor href attributes. The rendered output must match verbatim including the resolved href value.

trigger_keywords: what do all template engines render for the basic test case
what is the expected html output for the standard template
what does the email_template render with name Titus
hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
For FreeMarker, Mustache, and Pebble engines, the standard email_template merged with model {name: 'Titus'} must produce exactly: '

Ciao Titus!

'. Thymeleaf produces a slightly different structure without the doctype line and with an additional activation link paragraph.

trigger_keywords: how should the adapter read input
what is the execution adapter responsible for
how does the json command get dispatched to core library
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A thin execution adapter reads JSON commands from stdin line by line (one JSON object per line), dispatches each to the appropriate core library method based on the 'op' field, and writes the result to stdout. The core library must have no knowledge of JSON, stdin, or stdout. The adapter is the only component that handles serialization/deserialization and the op-to-method routing.

trigger_keywords: how are recipients formatted in output
how are multiple recipients joined in the output string
what separator is used between email addresses in output
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Multiple recipients within the same class (to, cc, bcc) are joined with a semicolon (';') in the output. For example, two primary recipients appear as 'to=roberto@gmail.com;titus@de.natura'. When a recipient class is absent (no cc or bcc supplied) the line still appears with an empty value, e.g., 'cc=' and 'bcc='.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs
what features have test cases
show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

trigger_keywords: what are the valid op field values
what values can op take
list all supported op values
what actions does the dispatcher handle
which op strings does the adapter support
what are the possible op inputs
tell me all the op names
what operations are supported
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The dispatcher routes on the 'op' field. Valid values are: 'email.build', 'inline_picture.build', 'attachment.build', 'image_type.list', 'image_type.get', 'mime.assemble', 'template.merge'. Each value maps to one feature.

context_pointers.vague_reference: similar to how we handled the validation in that user-profile module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The missing-required-field validation pattern used in Email, InlinePicture, and Attachment value objects: each mandatory field is checked at construction time and failure produces exactly two output lines — 'error=missing_required_field' and 'field=<fieldname>' — as specified in features 1, 2, and 3 of the PRD and their corresponding test cases.

The ImageType enum/catalog defined for Feature 4, which enumerates GIF, JPG, JPEG, and PNG with their extensions and MIME types in a fixed order. This catalog is also referenced by InlinePicture (Feature 2) to resolve content_type from an imageType name.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the logic-less one, one does inheritance, and there's that natural-templating one
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The three html-extension template engines are: Mustache (logic-less, feature 6.2), Pebble (inheritance-capable, feature 6.3), and Thymeleaf (natural-templating with attribute-level expressions, feature 6.4). All three use the 'html' extension but are distinct engines with different template files and placeholder syntaxes.

context_pointers.vague_reference: wrong extensions or blank names should fail clearly, and broken templates should also have their own distinct failure mode
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Two distinct error codes: 'error=invalid_template_name' (for blank/whitespace-only template references or wrong file extension) and 'error=template_processing_failed' (for templates that exist but cannot be parsed or compiled). Both must also emit 'engine=<enginename>' as the second line.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific details on that aspect right now — could you check the existing codebase or the test cases in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the exact contract? The test JSON files are the authoritative black-box spec for each feature's expected behavior.

feature1_email_model.json.description: Compose a logical email value object from its parts. Sender, at least one primary recipient, subject and body are mandatory; omitting any of them is reported as a missing-required-field error naming the absent part. Supplied recipients and custom headers are echoed back; when no custom headers are given the email exposes an empty header set.

cases.expected_output:
from=marco@urbs.aeterna
to=roberto@gmail.com;titus@de.natura
cc=info@de.natura
bcc=
subject=Laelius de amicitia
body=Firmamentum autem stabilitatis.
custom_header_count=2
custom_header.K1=V1
custom_header.K2=V2

from=marco@urbs.aeterna
to=roberto@gmail.com
cc=
bcc=
subject=Hello
body=Body text
custom_header_count=0

feature2_inline_picture.json.description: Describe an image to be embedded inline in an email body and referenced from a template placeholder. The descriptor requires three coordinates - the image kind, the backing file path, and the template placeholder name - and exposes the resolved MIME content type for the chosen image kind. Omitting any coordinate is reported as a missing-required-field error.

feature3_attachment.json.description: Wrap binary content as a named email attachment. Name and data are mandatory. The attachment exposes its bytes as a readable resource and resolves a MIME content type: an explicitly supplied type is honoured, otherwise the type is auto-detected from the byte content together with the file name. Omitting the data is reported as a missing-required-field error.

feature4_image_type.json.description: Expose the catalog of inline image kinds supported by the library. Each kind maps to a lowercase file extension and a wire MIME content type. The whole catalog can be listed, or a single kind can be looked up by name.

cases.expected_output:
GIF extension=gif content_type=image/gif
JPG extension=jpg content_type=image/jpeg
JPEG extension=jpeg content_type=image/jpeg
PNG extension=png content_type=image/png

feature5_mime_message.json.description: Assemble a logical email into a transport MIME message and report the framework-observable headers it produced. Sender, reply-to, every recipient class (primary, carbon-copy, blind-copy), subject and body are mapped onto the message; read-receipt and disposition-notification headers are emitted when requested; arbitrary custom headers are passed through. The message is marked multipart exactly when one or more attachments are present.

cases.expected_output:
from=cicero@mala.currunt
reply_to=tullius@urbs.aeterna
to=roberto@gmail.com
cc=tito@de.natura
bcc=caius@urbs.aeterna
subject=Laelius de amicitia
multipart=false
body=Firmamentum autem stabilitatis.
header_return_receipt=caligola@urbs.aeterna
header_disposition_notification_to=caligola@urbs.aeterna
custom_header.key1=value1
custom_header.key2=value2

from=cicero@mala.currunt
reply_to=cicero@mala.currunt
to=roberto@gmail.com
cc=
bcc=
subject=With files
multipart=true
header_return_receipt=
header_disposition_notification_to=

feature6_1_template_freemarker.json.description: Render a server-side HTML template using the expression-and-directive engine whose templates carry the 'ftl' extension. A valid template merged with a model substitutes the model values and returns the rendered document verbatim. A blank template reference or one whose extension is not 'ftl' is rejected as an invalid template name; a syntactically broken template surfaces as a template-processing failure.

feature6_2_template_mustache.json.description: Render a server-side HTML template using the logic-less mustache engine whose templates carry the 'html' extension. A valid template merged with a model substitutes the model values and returns the rendered document verbatim. A blank template reference or one whose extension is not 'html' is rejected as an invalid template name; a template that cannot be loaded or compiled surfaces as a template-processing failure.

feature6_3_template_pebble.json.description: Render a server-side HTML template using the inheritance-capable engine whose templates carry the 'html' extension. A valid template merged with a model substitutes the model values and returns the rendered document verbatim. A blank template reference or one whose extension is not 'html' is rejected as an invalid template name; a syntactically broken template surfaces as a template-processing failure.

feature6_4_template_thymeleaf.json.description: Render a server-side HTML template using the natural-templating engine whose templates carry the 'html' extension and support attribute-level expressions for both text content and hyperlink targets. A valid template merged with a model substitutes the model values and returns the rendered document verbatim. A blank template reference or one whose extension is not 'html' is rejected as an invalid template name.

cases.expected_output:
Ciao Titus!

Click Here for activaing your account
